sections.paragraph:
χωνευτός, ή, όν (χωνεύω ‘melt, pour metal’; Polyb. 34, 9, 11; Diod S 5, 35, 4; Plut., Lucull. 517 [37, 5]; schol. on Nicander, Ther. 257; ApcEsdr 4:43 p. 29, 22 Tdf. χωνεύσω τὴν γῆν; Just., A I, 9, 2) pert. to receiving shape or form through pouring of metal into a mold, cast, poured (LXX; ViElisha 3 [p. 95, 5 Sch.]; Eupolem. [II b.c.]: 723 Fgm. 2, 34, 7 Jac. p. 676 [in Eus., PE 9, 34, 9]; Jos., Ant. 8, 77) subst. τὸ χωνευτόν an image made of cast metal (Philo, Leg. All. 3, 36) B 12:6 (Dt 27:15).—DELG s.v. χέω.

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

schol. schol. = scholion, scholia, scholiast

Nicander Nicander , III/II b.c.—List 5

ApcEsdr ApcEsdr = Apocalypse of Esdras—List 2

Tdf. Tdf. = CvTischendorf—List 1, beg.

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Sch. Sch. = TSchermann, Prophetarum vitae fabulosae—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

Eus. Eus. = Eusebius of Caesarea, various works; IV a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

subst. subst. = substantive(ly)

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1093
